title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3,601.70 USD/T on April 27, 2026, down 0.04% from the previous day. Aluminum futures in the UK held above $3,600 per tonne, near a more than four-year high due to the continued blockage of the Strait of Hormuz threatening Middle Eastern supply disruptions. Prices are expected to reach 3643.69 USD/Tonne by quarter-end.

title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Global aluminum prices edged up 0.04% on April 26, 2026, stabilizing amid Middle East supply shocks. Traders balance disruptions from strikes on Gulf smelters and EGA facility shutdown against macroeconomic headwinds. Strict Chinese production caps and high energy costs keep inventories low, supporting elevated prices.

title: Price of Aluminum per Pound
summary: Live aluminum price on April 26, 2026, at 20:00 NY Time was 1.6524 USD per pound, down 0.08%. Charts and spot prices from New York, London, Hong Kong, and Sydney markets reflect ongoing base metals trading. Provides real-time updates on aluminum market dynamics.

title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
summary: The United States faces a severe aluminum supply crisis, with Bloomberg warning that supplies could hit zero within 30 days due to a 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum. This tariff, intended to protect domestic production, has triggered disruptions threatening U.S. manufacturing. Middle East production issues and a potential 1.5 million ton deficit exacerbate the shortage.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock Raises Risks For Global Metals Markets
summary: An aluminum supply shock is reshaping global metals markets amid geopolitical risks, trade policy shifts, and energy prices. Senior Metals Demand Analyst Natalie Scott-Gray highlights tightening aluminum supply, tin disruptions, and weakening copper demand. The discussion covers the 2026 commodities outlook.

title: Supply crunch drives aluminum to four year high
summary: A supply crunch has driven aluminum prices to a four-year high, as discussed by Oxford Economics lead economist Stephen Hare. The video examines the current state and outlook for aluminum markets amid ongoing pressures.

title: Industry Data - Weekly Raw Steel Production
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,848,000 net tons in the week ending April 18, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 80.0 percent. Production breakdown by districts includes North East: 133k tons, Great Lakes: 525k tons, Midwest: 295k tons, Southern: 823k tons, and Western: 72k tons.

title: US Steel Production Surges, Poised to Replace Imports
summary: US raw steel production increased 8.2% year-over-year, with capacity utilization at 79.1%. Industry leader Barry Zekelman highlights competitiveness from cheap resources and over $40 billion in investments, enabling displacement of imports and job creation.

title: China's Crude Steel Production Up Again in Mid-April
summary: China's steel production continued to increase at a slower pace in mid-April 2026. This update reflects ongoing trends in the global steel market amid various economic factors.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper rose to 6.04 USD/Lbs on April 27, 2026, marking a 0.28% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have risen 10.30%, and year-to-date gains stand at 24.44%. The market shows upward momentum amid recent stability near $5.5 per pound.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart aims to resume normal operations swiftly.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group aligned with mining firms in Congo over illicit mineral operations. This development impacts the copper supply chain, particularly from one of the world's major copper-producing regions.

title: BHP now expects nearly 2M tonnes copper production after record Escondida throughput
summary: BHP has revised its copper production expectations upward to nearly 2 million tonnes following record throughput at its Escondida mine. This represents a significant capacity achievement for one of the world's largest copper producers.

title: Goldman Sachs maintains 2026 copper price forecast and surplus projections
summary: Goldman Sachs has maintained its average copper price forecast at $12,650 per tonne for 2026. The forecast reflects expectations for market surplus conditions in the coming months.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto has produced the first copper cathode using its proprietary Nuton bioleaching technology at Gunnison Copper’s Johnson Camp mine, after over 30 years of research. The technology relies on microorganisms grown on site and is expected to achieve a mine-to-metal carbon footprint of 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper, the lowest in the U.S. The deployment targets production of approximately 30,000 tonnes of refined copper over a four-year demonstration period.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: CSIRO highlights copper's vital role in a sustainable clean energy future through efficient electricity transmission that minimizes energy losses. The industry's emphasis on recycling, resource efficiency, and technological innovation ensures copper remains a sustainable enabler for the energy transition. Copper is essential for critical technologies like mobile phones, electric vehicles, and digital infrastructure.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: As the world transitions to a net-zero future, copper's efficiency in transmitting electricity is crucial for minimizing energy losses. Copper is a key critical mineral for advanced technologies including mobile phones, electric vehicles, and digital infrastructure. The article underscores copper's importance in rewiring for sustainability.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel rose to 19,184.50 USD/T on April 27, 2026, up 0.31% from the previous day, with futures trading above $19,200 at a near two-year high. Supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability, including mining quota cuts in Indonesia and a sulfur shortage linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions, reinforce bullish momentum. Later update shows price at 19,291 USD/T, up 0.87%.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Nickel prices remain flat globally and in China due to weekend market closures, but the underlying price floor is supported by Indonesia's revisions to nickel ore benchmark pricing and slashed output quotas. International Nickel Study Group projections indicate a shift toward a global market deficit, combined with robust electric vehicle demand, keeping the medium-term outlook firm.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: Nickel futures are currently at 19,231.25, with a previous close of 19,113.75. Despite recent production cuts, UBS warns of a persistent surplus in the global nickel market through 2026, maintaining a bearish outlook.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices rose to 19,184.50 USD/T on April 27, 2026, up 0.31% from the previous day, reaching a near two-year high due to supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability. Mining quota cuts in Indonesia have constrained ore supply, raising concerns for downstream processors. A sulfur shortage from Strait of Hormuz disruptions has increased processing costs for nickel intermediates.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: The world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, PT Weda Bay Nickel, has been notified to cut its ore production quota to 12 million tons this year from 42 million tons in 2025, as the country aims to boost global prices of its key export. The company intends to apply for a revision of the quota. Nickel prices climbed last month following Indonesia's plans to reduce mine output.

title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs
summary: Prysmian shares rose as much as 4% on Wednesday due to expectations that the world's largest cable maker's substantial US business will benefit from US tariffs on copper. The company sources copper locally in the US, enabling it to pass on higher metal prices to customers. This positions Prysmian favorably amid the tariff developments.

title: Copper Cables: Antitrust Launches Investigation into Alleged Cartel, Prysmian and Nexans Stock Market Drops
summary: Italian antitrust authorities have launched an investigation into major low-voltage copper cable producers, including Prysmian Cavi e Sistemi Italia Srl, for alleged price-fixing since 2005. Inspections were conducted yesterday at company offices, distributors, and associations, with Prysmian shares dropping 1.26% and Nexans down 1.9% in response. Analysts view the impact on Prysmian as negligible, as the Italian low-voltage cable market is less than 3% of its revenues.
